$comment: First-party curated marketplace catalog (CP-FR-020 issue #621; CP-FR-021 issue #622; per-plugin subdir sources + signing-key rotation, issue #750). The catalog is a SIGNED envelope: `signature` is a base64 detached ed25519 signature over the canonical bytes of `payload` (see server/services/marketplace-integrity.ts), verified at load against the bundled first-party public key. The server fails closed (zero listings) on any signature mismatch. Each entry's `source.directory` names the subdirectory of the cloned repo that holds the plugin package (the monorepo-subdir model, #750): the installer clones `source.url`, stages only that subdirectory, and installs it. Each entry carries an `integrity` content digest (sha256-<hex> over the staged subdirectory), checked after staging and before commit; `revoked: true` removes an entry from listings and blocks install/update. The five installable entries (database, process, github-com, ghe, jira-self-hosted) carry REAL digests of their `plugins/<id>` subdirectory at the signed commit, validated end-to-end through the actual verifyPackageIntegrity path by a no-network CI test (server/services/marketplace-integrity.test.ts, issue #689); `redis` is revoked pending a published source and `worker-queue` is revoked (withdrawn). Because a digest binds to live `plugins/<id>` content, any change to those directories requires recomputing the digest and RE-SIGNING this file (the #689 test fails closed on drift). `verified` is a display-only curation flag. There is no third-party submission path: entries are added by maintainers only. The signing key was rotated in #750 (the prior key was lost); the matching ed25519 private key is held out of band and never committed. After editing this file you MUST re-sign it (pipe the ed25519 private-key PEM in on stdin): npx tsx server/scripts/sign-marketplace-catalog.ts < /path/to/ed25519-priv.pem

entries.name: Database
entries.summary: Docker-backed databases via compose, with migrations and connection-string templating.

entries.name: Process
entries.summary: Long-running commands: dev servers, watchers, and workers, with setup and env handling.

entries.name: Redis
entries.summary: A Redis cache component: brings up a Redis container and exposes its connection string. Revoked: no published source yet.

entries.name: GitHub.com
entries.summary: Connect GitHub issues, projects, and code-scanning alerts to your benches.

entries.name: GitHub Enterprise
entries.summary: Integration for self-hosted GitHub Enterprise Server: PAT plus instance URL auth with optional self-signed TLS opt-in.

entries.name: Self-hosted Jira
entries.summary: Integration for self-hosted Atlassian Jira (Data Center 8.14+) with PAT auth and configurable link-type mapping.

entries.name: Worker Queue
entries.summary: A background worker-queue component. Revoked: withdrawn by Roubo maintainers.
